title: Singing Mountain Clan
content:
{{Top|canon=Singing Mountain (clan)}}
{{Otheruses|Singing Mountain}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:Singing_Mountain_Clan_symbol.png]]
|name=Singing Mountain Clan
|type=[[Witches of Dathomir|Datomirian Witch]] clan<ref name="TCoPL">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref>
|founder=
|leader=*[[Augwynne Djo]]<ref name="TCoPL" />
*[[Aujante K'lee]]<ref name="SWG:AToTC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|A Tale of Two Clans|Dathomir}}</ref>
|subgroup=[[Council of Sisters]]<ref name="TCoPL" />
|headquarters=*[[Singing Mountain Clan Stronghold]]<ref name="TCoPL" />
*[[Singing Mountain Clan Village]]<ref name="TCoPL" />
|locations=[[Singing Mountain (Dathomir)/Legends|Singing Mountain]], [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]<ref name="TCoPL" />
|formed=
|founded=
|fragmented=
|reorganized=
|dissolved=
|restored=
|affiliation=[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]<ref name="CTD">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''</ref>
}}
{{Quote|The Witches of the Singing Mountain should not be underestimated; while their weapons might appear simple their spells make them formidable opponents. Though not as hostile as the Nightsisters, the Witches of the Singing Mountain won't show you any mercy if they see you as a threat.|[[Rubina]]|<ref name="SWG:Walkabout">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Walkabout|Dathomir}}</ref>}}
The '''Singing Mountain Clan''' was a [[Witches of Dathomir|Dathomiri Witch clan]] based at the [[Singing Mountain (Dathomir)/Legends|Singing Mountain]] on [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]. The clan appeared as a cohesive, hierarchical matriarchy defined by its warrior culture and magical tradition.

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it was a bitter rival of the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] as their leader [[Gethzerion]] used to be part of the Singing Mountain Clan before she was exiled for attempting to kill the Clan Mother [[Augwynne Djo]]. 

==Description==
{{Quote|Never concede to evil. That is our oldest and most sacred law. When we concede to evil, even in a small way, we feed it, and it grows stronger.|[[Augwynne Djo]]|<ref name="TCoPL" />}}
The Singing Mountain Clan was a clan of [[Witches of Dathomir]]. The clan was a matriarchal community in which political authority, ritual leadership, and martial power were held by the clan's women, particularly those with [[Dathomir Magic|spellcasting]] abilities. The clan was governed by a [[Clan mother/Legends|clan mother]], who presided over a [[Council of Sisters]] that directed decisions concerning internal affairs, justice, diplomacy, and war.<ref name="TCoPL" />

[[File:SingingMountainGuardian-GH.jpg|thumb|240px|left|A Singing Mountain Clan warrior wearing a traditional outfit with the clan symbol]]
The clan's internal life was structured around ancient traditions and rituals, strict ethical codes and guided by principles of collective defense. [[Allya/Legends|Allya]]'s [[Book of Law/Legends|Book of Law]] provided the moral and legal framework of the community, guiding responses to threats such as [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] and outsiders. Like in many societies living in a close community, the witches were wary of strangers. Nevertheless, they could offer sanctuary to offworlders, granting them equal status under clan protection once accepted.<ref name="TCoPL" />
[[File:Singing_Mountain_Sister.jpg|thumb|240px|right|A Singing Mountain Clan sister riding a rancor]]

Women of the clan considered themselves as [[Sister (Witches of Dathomir)|sisters]]. Most of them were fierce warriors dedicated to the defense of their clan and their territory. These warriors included witches armed with [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] or mounted on [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]] but those with spellcasting talent held the primary positions of influence. Like in other witch clans on the planet, men and children lived within the community but occupied a subordinate position under the protection of the witches and had to obey established restrictions. Males were traditionally regarded as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] or breeders. Men outside the clan could be taken as husbands or slaves by witches with the power to capture them and claim them as their own.<ref name="TCoPL" />

Th clan was named after the [[Singing Mountain (Dathomir)/Legends|Singing Mountain]], the mountain inhabited by the clan. The witches had established their [[Singing Mountain Clan Village|village]] and their agriculture lands across the lower slopes of the mountain and in the valley. The village functioned in direct relationship to the [[Singing Mountain Clan Stronghold|fortress]], which dominated the landscape from its higher position on the flank of the mountain. The two formed a unified settlement, the village serving as the living base of the population and the fortress acting as the defensive, ceremonial, and political center.<ref name="TCoPL" />

==History==
===Old Republic era===
{{Quote|Over time, the daughters of Allya have become known to outworlders as the Witches of Dathomir and have splintered into a number of smaller clans. The largest of these is the clan of the Singing Mountain.|[[Rubina]]|<ref name="SWG:Rubina">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=conversation|Rubina|Dathomir}}</ref>}}
[[File:Augwynne_Djo.jpg|left|thumb|200px|Clan Mother Augwynne Djo]]
The Singing Mountain Clan was one of the many Datomirian Witch clan formed when the earlier followers of [[Allya]] splintered into a number of smaller clans.<ref name="SWG:Rubina" />

The Singing Mountain was located close to the [[Infinity Plain]] and its ancient [[Kwa/Legends|Kwa]] [[Star Temple]]s.<ref name="TLJ">[[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']]</ref> In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]],<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] [[Mission to Dathomir (Post–Naboo crisis)|thwarted]] a plot of the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] to used an ancient [[Infinity Gate|superweapon]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Later, due to the proximity between the Star Temple containing this weapon and the Singing Mountain Clan Stronghold, the Singing Mountain Clan ''de facto'' became the guardian of the Star Temple. Following the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jax Pavan]], a [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] survivor, visited the Singing Mountain Clan in order to obtain the support of [[Clan mother/Legends|Clan Mother]] [[Augwynne Djo]] to access the Star Temple. Djo and other witches also helped Pavan to open a [[SD41263.I|Sith holocron]] in order to learn the secrets of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Ramage]]. Several days later, after a successful [[Infiltration of Kantaros Station|mission]] to rescue a [[Whiplash|rebel]] [[Thi Xon Yimmon|leader]], Jax Pavan returned to Dathomir to have his mind cleared of his dark knowledge by Augwynne Djo.<ref name="TLJ" />

===War with the Nightsisters===
{{Quote|The Singing Mountain clan is in constant conflict with the Nightsisters, another clan of Force-users here on Dathomir.|Augwynne Djo|<ref name="SWGTCG:TNR">{{SWGTCG|set=The Nightsister's Revenge|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}}</ref>}}
Although the Nightsisters were mostly exterminated by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="Massacre">{{TCW|Massacre}}</ref> the evil witch sect was reformed prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="GatORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> by [[Gethzerion]], the daughter of Singing Mountain Clan Mother Augwynne Djo. Gethzerion and Djo's oldest daughter, [[Barukka]], had both turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and Gethzerion even attempted to murder her own mother. The plan was only foiled by the sacrifice of their youngest sister [[Kara'Teel]], who gave her life to protect her mother.<ref name="CTD" /> These terrible events led Gethzerion to become the leader of the Nightsisters. From this day, the Singing Mountain Clan and the Nightsisters became mortal enemies.<ref name="TCoPL" />

[[File:WitchBattle-TSS.png|thumb|right|220px|A Singing Mountain Clan witch defends herself against a Nightsister]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Singing Mountain Clan was the largest clan of Dathomir witches.<ref name="SWG:Rubina" /> Around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Galactic Empire [[Invasion of Dathomir|invaded]] Dathomir.<ref name="GatORW" /> Imperial troops initially encountered no resistance from the Witches. Howecer, Gethzerion's Nightsisters progressively [[Mind trick/Legends#Force corrupt|corrupted]] and took control of Imperial staff.<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|65|Who's Who in Star Wars Galaxies|Who's Who in ''Star Wars'' Galaxies}}</ref> Realizing that the Nightsisters were more powerful than what he expected, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] ordered the Witches of Dathomir to be confined on their planet.<ref name="TDSS">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> The planet was soon [[Blockade of Dathomir|blockaded]] by Imperial starships for the next years.<ref name="CTD" /> The blockade caused the Singing Mountain Clan to hate the Empire.<ref name="SWGTCG">{{SWGTCG|set=Galactic Hunters|cardname=Singing Mountain Councilwoman}}</ref>

Following the Battle of Yavin, the conflict between the Singing Mountain Clan and the Nightsisters became extremely violent.<ref name="SWG:AToTC" /> At that time, the clan was led by Clan Mother [[Aujante K'lee]].<ref name="SWG:AToTC" /> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> a large [[Nightsister vs. Singing Mountain Clan Battle|battle]] took place in [[Asou Dankal]] between warriors of the two groups, involving spell casters and rancors.<ref name="SWG:NvSMCB">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=poi|Nightsister vs. Singing Mountain Clan Battle|Dathomir}}</ref> The same year, [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and adventurers traveled to Dathomir and offered their services to both the Singing Mountain Clan and the Nightsisters,<ref name="SWG:TttSM">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Travel to the Singing Mountain|Dathomir}}</ref><ref name="SWG:TttNS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Travel to the Nightsister Stronghold|Dathomir}}</ref> joining the war between the two clans on both sides.<ref name="SWG:AToTC" /> The spacers loyal to the Singing Mountain Clan also completed a series of missions for the clan. They captured the Singing Mountain Clan servant [[Aldiae]] who was about to defect to the Nightsisters. They then rescued the witch [[Jhuryx]] captured by the Nightsisters. They also killed the Nightsister leader [[Morathax]].<ref name="SWG:AK">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Aujante K'lee|Dathomir}}</ref> The spacers later prevented the Nightsisters to use the [[Berserker Virus]],<ref name="SWG:Izaryx">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Izaryx|Dathomir}}</ref> a [[Bioweapon/Legends|bioweapon]] developed for the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Borvo]],<ref name="SWG:KM">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Kritus Morven|Dathomir}}</ref> against the Singing Mountain Clan rancors.<ref name="SWG:Izaryx" /> The offworlders also defeated the Nightsister [[Kyrisa]]<ref name="SWG:TGG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|The Greater Good|Dathomir}}</ref> who was responsible for the creation of mentally unstable and highly aggressive mutant rancors.<ref name="SWG:TWT">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|The Whole Truth|Dathomir}}</ref> Augwynne Djo and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Rachi Sitra]] attempted to capture Kyrisa thereafter but the latter managed to escape the planet.<ref name="SWGTCG:TNR">{{SWGTCG|set=The Nightsister's Revenge|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}}</ref> In addition, the spacers discovered the terrible fate of the [[Howling Crag Clan]], decimated by the [[Imperial bioweapons Project I71A|Imperial bioweapon]] during the [[Blackwing virus outbreak on Dathomir]].<ref name="SWG:Recon">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Recon|Dathomir}}</ref> In the meantime, the Nightsisters also undertook assaults against the Singing Mountain Clan.<ref name="SWG:AToTC" /> Sisters [[Selia]] and [[Espernia]] were killed by the Nightsisters.<ref name="SWG:Vurlene">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Vurlene|Dathomir}}</ref><ref name="SWG:Diax">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Diax|Dathomir}}</ref> Later, a group of spacers working for Gethzerion even assassinated Augwynne Djo's adopted daughters [[Aelta]] and [[Chastina]].<ref name="SWG:Gethzerion">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Gethzerion|Dathomir}}</ref>

[[File:Singing_Mountain_Clan_battle.jpg|thumb|left|280px|A group of Singing Mountain Clan witches and rancors preparing to battle Nightsisters]]
Beyond the Nightsister clan itself, the Singing Mountain Clan was also in conflict with Nightsister outcasts such as the [[Spiderclan/Legends|Spiderclan]], a group of heretical Nightsisters who preferred taming [[gaping spider]]s than rancors.<ref name="SWG:Recon">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Recon|Dathomir}}</ref> The spacers working for the Singing Mountain Clan eventually destroyed the Queen Mother of the [[Spiderclan/Legends|Spiderclan]], which was in fact a giant gaping spider.<ref name="SWG:THP">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|The Hunting Party|Dathomir}}</ref> The offworlders also had to deal with [[Nandina]],<ref name="SWGTCG:NRps">{{SWGTCG|set=The Nightsister's Revenge|scenario=Nightsister Roundup promotional scenario}}</ref> a follower of the Nightsister outcast [[Axkva Min]]<ref name="SWG:AM">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=heroic|Axkva Min}}</ref> who had been imprisoned by Gethzerion in the [[Chamber of Banishment]] because she had became a threat to her own people.<ref name="SWG:TNH">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|The Nightsister Headdress|Dathomir}}</ref> In 1 ABY,<ref name="SWG_timeline" /> Axkva Min and her followers discovered a way to free themselves from her confinement but they were killed when attempting to escape.<ref name="SWG:AM" /> The only survivor was Nandina. After escaping the Chamber of Banishment, she tried to assassinate Arch Witch [[Azzenaj]] of the Singing Mountain Clan but was unsuccessful. Nandina and her rancor [[Gorvo]] were finally killed by spacers loyal to the Singing Mountain Clan.<ref name="SWGTCG:NRps" />

===Reunited with the galaxy===
{{Quote|We have all agreed to help Leia and the offworlders.|Augwynne Djo|<ref name="TCoPL" />}}
[[File:Luke_Teneniel-ERC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Teneniel Djo fighting Luke Skywalker]]
After the death of Emperor Palpatine at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] replaced the Galactic Empire as the main galactic government. In the following years, [[Warlord of the Empire|Imperial Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] emerged as a major threat to the New Republic.<ref name="CTD" /> The former controlled the [[Dathomir system/Legends|Dathomir system]] and stationed a fleet in orbit, maintaining the Imperial blockade of the planet.<ref name="TCoPL" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], during the [[Hunt for Zsinj|New Republic campaign]] against Zsinj, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] won the deed to the planet Dathomir in a card game and subsequently abducted [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] to the planet aboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] in order to prevent her marriage to [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]] of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]]. After a skirmish with Zsinj's forces in orbit, the ''Millennium Falcon'' crash-landed on the planet. Solo, Organa and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] soon encountered the witches of the Singing Mountain Clan and took shelter in their village. Shortly thereafter, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and Prince Isolder arrived on the planet, searching for Princess Leia. Both men were later captured by [[Teneniel Djo]], the grand-daughter of Clan Mother Augwynne Djo. Teneniel claimed Isolder as her mate and saw in Luke a rare male witch that claimed as a slave. All the offworlders were eventually reunited in the village of the Singing Mountain Clan. Augwynne Djo offered them protection but the witches regarded the newcomers with suspicion.<ref name="TCoPL" />

[[File:IsolderRancor.jpg|thumb|left|210px|Isolder and the Heroes of Yavin riding rancors with the Singing Mountain Clan]]
The situation escalated when Zsinj discovered the presence of the New Republic heroes on the planet. The warlord made a deal with Gethzerion, offering the Nightsisters a ship to escape the blockaded planet in exchange for turning over Han Solo. The ineluctable [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|battle]] erupted when the Nightsisters attacked the Singing Mountain fortress to capture the offworlders. The latter allied with the Singing Mountain Clan and helped the witches to repel the Nightsisters and their Imperial allies. After a series of confrontations marked by magical and armed combat, the Nightsisters were finally defeated. Frustrated by this outcome, Warlord Zsinj betrayed Gethzerion and destroyed her escaping shuttle from orbit, killing instantaneously the leader of the Nightsisters and her last surviving followers. Zsinj then decided to punish the witches by unleashing siege weapons known as [[orbital nightcloak]]s against the planet. However, a space battle soon began when the [[Hapan Royal Navy]] arrived in the Dathomir system and attacked the Imperial fleet. The warlord himself was killed in the battle and his fleet was annihilated, along with the dreaded orbital nightcloaks.<ref name="TCoPL" />

[[File:BattleofDathomir.jpg|220px|thumb|right|The Singing Mountain Clan defend its stronghold against Nightsisters and their Imperial slaves.]]
As the battle ended, the witches of the Singing Mountain Clan had come to respect the offworlders, including males. The conflict resolved with Teneniel Djo marrying Isolder, creating an unexpected alliance between the "primitive" Dathomir and the wealthy Hapes Consortium.<ref name="TCoPL" /> Teneniel Djo would promptly become the new [[King/Legends|queen]] of [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]]. The destruction of the Nightsisters and [[Zsinj's Empire]] opened an era of peace for the Witches of Dathomir. Han Solo offered the planet's deed to Augwynne Djo who convinced the other clans to make Dathomir join the New Republic. The Singing Mountain Clan thus emerged as a bastion of tradition and resilience able to preserve its heritage while adapting to new political realities.<ref name="CTD" />

===Later history===
{{Quote|If any of you would like to learn the ways of the Force, to do what Vilas and I can do-whether you be male or female, noble-born or slave-come join us. Our settlement is at the bottom of the Great Canyon, only three days' journey from here by foot.|[[Vonnda Ra]], to the Singing Mountain Clan sisters|<ref name="YJKSA">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Shadow Academy]]''</ref>}}
In the years following the Battle of the Singing Mountain, several witches of the Singing Mountain Clan left Dathomir to join Luke Skywalker's [[New Jedi Order]], including [[Damaya]],<ref name="TUF">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref> [[Kirana Ti]] and her niece [[Sanola Ti]].<ref name="Fury">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref>

In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], the Empire returned to Dathomir, in the form of an [[Imperial Remnant]] expedition led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vit]]. The Imperials made an alliance with a group of Dathomiri who styled themselves as a reborn clan of Nightsister and overpowered the small New Republic force on the planet as well as the resisting Witches of Dathomir. Although the Infinity Gate — the main objective of the Empire — was inoperable, General Vit and his troops remained on the planet to impose Imperial authority and recruit Force-sensitive warriors for the service of the Empire. The Imperial garrison began incarcerating hostile witch clans, including the Singing Mountain Clan and the [[Dreaming River Clan]]. At this point, a group of New Republic sympathizers, including [[New Republic Special Forces/Legends|special forces]] officer and Jedi trainee [[Dal Konur]], as well as renegade Imperial [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]] [[Kir Kanos]], arrived on the planet. Working together with the Dathomiri Witch resistance, they fought their way though Imperial troops and finally secured the [[Dathomir communications center]]. From there, they were able to contact Queen Teneniel Djo of the Hapes Consortium and convinced her to send a Hapan fleet liberate the planet.<ref name="Nightsaber">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031120nightsaber|text=Nightsaber|int=Nightsaber}}</ref>

In [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], Jedi Master Luke Skywalker and Jedi apprentice [[Tenel Ka Djo]], the daughter of Teneniel Djo and Isolder, [[Second Imperium Crisis|traveled]] to Dathomir in order to request support of the Singing Mountain Clan Stronghold in searching information about resurgent Nightsisters allied with an Imperial faction known as the [[Second Imperium]]. They discovered that the [[Great Canyon Clan]] had became Nightsisters under the leadership of [[Tamith Kai]]. These Nightsisters attempted to recruit new followers among other clans, including the Singing Mountain Clan. Posing as Singing Mountain Clan members willing to join the Nightsisters, two Jedi used this opportunity to infiltrate the Great Canyon Clan, and subsequently the Second Imperium's [[Shadow Academy]].<ref name="YJKSA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Singing Mountain Clan first appeared in the [[1994]] novel ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'', written by [[Dave Wolverton]].<ref name="TCoPL" /> The clan was later featured in the novels ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Shadow Academy]]'' and [[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']].<ref name="YJKSA" /><ref name="TLJ" />

[[File:Singing_Mountain_Clan_concept.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Concept art of various Singing Mountain Clan witches for ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''.]]
The Singing Mountain Clan also appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:NvSMCB" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> The Singing Mountain Clan was a major feature of the game as it was one of the two factions of non-players characters on Dathomir, the other being the Nightsisters.<ref name="FitG">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68996&month=112010|text=Friday Feature – Factions in the Galaxy}}</ref> The game was the first visual media to present the Singing Mountain Clan witches in their environment and thereby developed an authentic visual atmosphere for the clan, including original clothing and architecture.<ref name="FPtP">''[[From Pencil to Pixel: The Art of Star Wars Galaxies]]''</ref> Upon the original release of the game, players had the possibility to visit the Singing Mountain Clan Stronghold and work for the witches of the clan.<ref name="SWG:SMCS">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=location|Singing Mountain Clan Stronghold|Dathomir}}</ref> They could also choose instead to align with the Nightsisters against the Singing Mountain Clan.<ref name="SWG:NS">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=location|Nightsister Stronghold|Dathomir}}</ref> These quests series were removed from the game upon the release of the revamped "[[W:c:swg:Witches of Dathomir Theme Park|Witches of Dathomir Theme Park]]," introduced in the [[W:c:swg:Game Update 19|Game Update 19]] on [[December 14]], [[2010]].<ref name="GU19">{{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/posts/list.m?topic_id=1160720|text=''Star Wars'' Galaxies: Game Update 19}}</ref> The new "Theme Park" proposed modernized quests in which the players could also choose to join either the Singing Mountain Clan or the Nightsisters.<ref name="SWG:TttSM" /><ref name="SWG:TttNS" />
